domestic_security.procedure_overview: Standard 2-tier security check at all terminals

steps.title: Document Check
steps.description: Show boarding pass and government-issued photo ID at entry gate

steps.title: Baggage X-Ray
steps.description: Place cabin bags on conveyor belt for X-ray scanning. Remove laptops and place separately.

steps.title: Personal Frisking
steps.description: Walk through metal detector. Random pat-down may follow.

steps.title: Gate Security
steps.description: Secondary hand baggage X-ray at boarding gate for select flights

prohibited_items.examples: Scissors, knives, razor blades, box cutters
Only 1 lighter allowed in pocket, not in bag
Water bottles, shampoo, perfume. Buy inside after security.
Must be in cabin baggage, not checked
Must carry in hand luggage only

allowed_with_restrictions.rule: Must be removed from bag and screened separately
Carry prescription; liquid medicines allowed in reasonable quantity with doctor's note
Allowed for infants; may be tested at checkpoint
Sealed in STEB bag with receipt. Only for international transfers.

additional_steps.title: Immigration Check
additional_steps.description: Passport and visa verification at immigration counter

additional_steps.title: Customs Declaration
additional_steps.description: Declare items exceeding duty-free allowance. Use Green channel if nothing to declare.

additional_steps.title: Random Screening
additional_steps.description: Additional random pat-down or bag search may be conducted

international_security.liquid_rule: 3-1-1 rule: Each container max 100ml, all containers in one 1-litre clear resealable bag
international_security.currency_limit: ₹25,000 cash for domestic. USD 5,000 or equivalent for international without declaration.

prohibited_items_strict.examples: Most fresh produce banned on international flights
Seeds, soil, live plants require phytosanitary certificate
Even decorative swords, replica guns are banned
Fake branded items can be confiscated

domestic_security.procedure_overview: Standard CISF security check at Terminal 1 and Terminal 2

steps.title: Entry Gate Check
steps.description: Show printed/digital boarding pass and valid photo ID (Aadhaar, Passport, DL)

steps.title: Baggage Screening
steps.description: All cabin bags go through X-ray. Remove power banks, laptops, and large electronics.

steps.title: Metal Detector & Frisking
steps.description: Walk through DFMD gate. CISF personnel conduct pat-down search.

steps.title: Boarding Gate Re-check
steps.description: Random re-screening of hand baggage at boarding gate

prohibited_items.examples: Scissors, knives, razor blades, nail cutters with blade
Max 1 lighter in person, none in checked bag
All liquids must be under 100ml in a clear bag
Must be in cabin baggage only; max 20,000mAh
All types of crackers, flares, and explosive materials
Cricket bats, hockey sticks must be checked in

allowed_with_restrictions.rule: Must be removed and screened separately
Carry original prescription; liquids need doctor's note
Baby food/milk allowed for infants traveling with you

additional_steps.title: Immigration
additional_steps.description: Passport and visa check at immigration counter. Keep boarding pass ready.

additional_steps.title: Customs
additional_steps.description: Green channel: nothing to declare. Red channel: goods exceeding ₹50,000 duty-free limit.

international_security.liquid_rule: 3-1-1 rule applies. One clear resealable bag per person.
international_security.currency_limit: ₹25,000 cash for domestic. USD 5,000 for international without RBI declaration.

prohibited_items_strict.examples: Most fresh produce banned on international flights
Seeds, soil, live plants require phytosanitary certificate
Even decorative swords, replica guns are banned
Fake branded items can be confiscated

domestic_security.procedure_overview: Modern security setup with automated tray retrieval system

steps.title: E-Gate / Document Check
steps.description: Scan DigiYatra face ID or show boarding pass with photo ID

steps.title: Smart Baggage Screening
steps.description: Place bags in automated tray. Laptops can stay inside in newer CT scanners.

steps.title: Body Scanner
steps.description: Walk through millimeter wave body scanner (no pat-down needed in most cases)

steps.title: Gate Check
steps.description: Quick ID verification at boarding gate

prohibited_items.examples: Knives, scissors, blades of any kind

allowed_with_restrictions.rule: May stay in bag with new CT scanners; follow staff instructions
Must be in sealed STEB bags with receipts

additional_steps.title: Immigration
additional_steps.description: Automated e-gates available for Indian passport holders

prohibited_items_strict.examples: Most fresh produce banned on international flights
Seeds, soil, live plants require phytosanitary certificate
Even decorative swords, replica guns are banned
Fake branded items can be confiscated

UAE.warning: No pork products allowed in any form
Prescription drugs (especially painkillers, sleeping pills) need a medical certificate from a UAE-accredited doctor
E-cigarettes and vaping devices are COMPLETELY BANNED. Possession can lead to arrest and fines up to AED 200,000
Alcohol can only be purchased at licensed shops; public intoxication is a criminal offense
Photography of government buildings, airports, military installations is prohibited

USA.warning: All electronics larger than a phone must be placed in separate bin
TSA PreCheck available for expedited screening with registration
CBP declaration form required for all arrivals. Declare food items.
Absolutely no agricultural products (fruits, meats, plants) without declaration

Singapore.warning: Chewing gum is PROHIBITED. Import and sale banned with heavy fines
Drug trafficking carries MANDATORY DEATH PENALTY. Zero tolerance policy.
Duty-free liquor: max 1 litre spirits, 1 litre wine, 1 litre beer
E-cigarettes and vaping devices are banned
Cigarettes must have 'SDPC' marking or face heavy fines

UK.warning: No food of animal origin from outside the EU/UK allowed
Marmite and other dense liquids may be flagged at security

Japan.warning: Strict drug laws - even trace amounts of marijuana can result in imprisonment
Some over-the-counter medicines (pseudoephedrine, codeine) are banned
Meat products require quarantine inspection certificate
Duty-free: 3 bottles of alcohol (760ml each), 200 cigarettes

Australia.warning: Extremely strict biosecurity. All food, plant, and animal products MUST be declared
Failure to declare food items can result in fines up to AUD 630,000
Wooden items and souvenirs may be inspected for pests

India.consequence: Currency seized + fine up to 3x the undeclared amount
Arrest, imprisonment up to 3 years under IPC Section 420
Arrest under NDPS Act, minimum 10 years imprisonment for drugs
Ban from airline, fine up to ₹15 lakh under DGCA rules

International.consequence: Heavy fines, confiscation, possible jail time
Deportation, ban on re-entry for 1-10 years
Up to 5 years imprisonment + heavy fines under CITES

tips: Arrive 2 hours early for domestic flights, 3 hours for international
Wear slip-on shoes to speed up screening
Keep electronics and liquids in an easily accessible pocket of your bag
When in doubt, ALWAYS declare items at customs — honesty prevents penalties
Download your airline app for a digital boarding pass backup
Take a photo of your checked baggage contents for insurance purposes
Keep medicines in original packaging with prescription labels visible
Check your destination country's customs website 24 hours before travel
Use DigiYatra at BLR and select DEL terminals for faster processing
Power banks MUST be in cabin baggage - never put them in checked luggage
